message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="pink" summary="Confirmed: nav link order and :has() behavior -- fully aligned">
Heydon, all great points. Two things I want to highlight:

**Nav link order**: Your proposed order (Inbox, Next Actions, Projects, Waiting For, Someday/Maybe, Review, Sign out) is exactly right. It follows GTD workflow priority AND means keyboard users reach the most important views first. The natural wrapping on mobile creates a visual hierarchy for free -- primary views on the top row, less-frequent views on the second row. No clever CSS needed, just correct source order. I'll make sure this ordering is documented for whoever implements the nav.

**`:has()` + `display: none` behavior**: Thank you for confirming the edge case analysis. The fact that `display: none` excludes values from form submission but preserves them in the DOM is exactly the behavior we want. Users can toggle between choices without losing their input, and the server only receives the fields relevant to the current selection. This is one of those cases where the platform does the right thing by default -- no JavaScript state management needed.

Steve Schoger is on board with header nav (he sent me a detailed revised design). Carson confirmed `hx-boost` on `<body>`. The team is fully aligned. Simpler HTML, simpler CSS, better accessibility -- the sweet spot, as you said.

Good collaboration on this. Your pushback on the sidebar prevented us from building the wrong thing.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Lea is confirming full alignment. No action needed -- just a brief acknowledgment.

input.content:
Lea, thanks for confirming and for documenting the nav link order. Good collaboration indeed -- the team converged on the right patterns quickly once we talked through the tradeoffs.

One small thing to document alongside the nav order: the Sign out action should be a `<form>` with a `<button>`, not an `<a>` link. It's a POST action that destroys session state -- that's a state mutation, not navigation. We already have this pattern in the current codebase (`templates/todos.html` line 7-8: `<form method="post" action="/logout">`). Just make sure whoever implements the new nav preserves this rather than converting it to a link for visual consistency with the other nav items.

Ready for implementation.
input.summary: Sign out should stay as form/button, not a link -- preserving current pattern

routing.summary: Sign out should stay as form/button, not a link -- preserving current pattern

content.text:
Brief but important detail flagged to Lea: Sign out must remain a `<form>` with `<button>` (POST action, state mutation), not be converted to an `<a>` link when the nav is redesigned. The current codebase already has this correct pattern in `templates/todos.html`.

All my review and advisory work for the product discovery task is complete. The team has 9/9 consensus. I'm standing by for implementation tasks.
